name: Social Security Administration
process_summary: Call 1-800-772-1213 or visit your local SSA office. Report the death and ask about survivor benefits. The funeral home may report the death automatically — confirm this. Any payment received for the month of death must be returned immediately.
mail_address: Contact your local Social Security office
important_notes: Social Security payments are issued for the prior month. A payment received after death likely covers the month of death and MUST be returned. Keeping this payment is a federal crime.

name: Medicare / Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services
process_summary: Call 1-800-633-4227. Medicare coverage ends on the date of death. Notify them to stop automatic payments. If the deceased was enrolled in a Medicare Advantage plan or Part D drug plan, notify those plans separately.
mail_address: Contact your local Social Security office (which handles Medicare enrollment)
important_notes: If Medicaid was also involved, notify your state Medicaid agency separately.

name: Internal Revenue Service (IRS)
process_summary: File the deceased's final Form 1040 by April 15 of the following year. Obtain an EIN for the estate using Form SS-4 (free, online at IRS.gov). If the estate earns income during administration (interest, rent, etc.), file Form 1041 annually.
mail_address: Varies by state — see IRS.gov for your filing location
typical_timeline: Final return due April 15 following year of death
important_notes: Write 'DECEASED' and the date of death at the top of the final Form 1040. A surviving spouse may file jointly for the year of death.

name: U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs
process_summary: Call 1-800-827-1000 or visit va.gov. Report the death to stop VA benefits (overpayments must be returned). Apply for burial benefits — eligible veterans can be buried in a national cemetery at no cost. Apply for DIC (Dependency and Indemnity Compensation) for surviving dependents.
typical_timeline: Burial benefits can be arranged quickly; DIC claims take 3–6 months
important_notes: VA provides a burial flag, Presidential Memorial Certificate, and headstone/marker for eligible veterans at no charge.

name: Facebook / Meta
process_summary: Visit Facebook's Special Request page to memorialize or remove the account. Memorialization turns the page into a memorial where friends can post memories. Removal permanently deletes the account. A Legacy Contact (if previously designated) can manage the memorial.
required_documents: Proof of relationship to the deceased
important_notes: Instagram (also owned by Meta) has a separate memorialization process at instagram.com/accounts/contact.

name: Google
process_summary: Use Google's Inactive Account Manager troubleshooter to submit a request. Google may grant access to certain data (Gmail, Drive, Photos) to immediate family with proper documentation. Alternatively, request account deletion.
required_documents: Proof of authority (letters testamentary, if requesting content)
important_notes: Google cannot provide passwords. They may provide data downloads in some cases with proper legal documentation.

name: Apple
process_summary: If the deceased designated a Digital Legacy contact with an access key, that person can request access at digital-legacy.apple.com. Otherwise, Apple requires a court order granting access to the account. Apple purchases (apps, music, movies) are non-transferable.
required_documents: Digital Legacy access key (if set up)
mail_address: Apple Inc., One Apple Park Way, Cupertino, CA 95014
important_notes: Apple ID purchases cannot be transferred. iCloud data may be accessible with proper legal authority.

name: Amazon
process_summary: Contact Amazon customer service by phone or chat. Request account closure and cancellation of all subscriptions including Prime. Check for any gift card balances — Amazon may issue a check. Kindle books and other digital purchases are non-transferable.
mail_address: Amazon.com, Inc., 410 Terry Ave. North, Seattle, WA 98109
important_notes: Check for active subscriptions, Alexa devices, and AWS accounts if the deceased was a developer.

name: PayPal / Venmo
process_summary: Contact PayPal's estate team by calling 1-888-221-1161. They will freeze the account and initiate a transfer of any remaining balance to the estate. The process requires documentation and typically results in a mailed check. Venmo is owned by PayPal and should be reported together.
mail_address: PayPal, Inc., 2211 North First Street, San Jose, CA 95131
important_notes: Any pending transactions will be canceled. Business PayPal accounts may have additional requirements.

name: Bank / Credit Union
process_summary: Visit the branch in person with certified death certificates and your letters testamentary. The bank will freeze individual accounts and work with you to transfer funds to the estate account or directly to beneficiaries if POD (payable on death) designations exist. Individual accounts without beneficiaries go through probate.
required_documents: Letters testamentary or small estate affidavit
important_notes: Joint accounts typically pass directly to the surviving joint owner. Check each account for POD designations — these transfer outside probate.

name: Brokerage / Investment Firm
process_summary: Contact the brokerage's estate services department. If the deceased named beneficiaries on the accounts (TOD — transfer on death), those assets pass directly to beneficiaries without probate. Otherwise, the brokerage will require letters testamentary to release assets to the estate.
important_notes: Do not liquidate investments without consulting a tax advisor — selling inherited assets may trigger capital gains taxes. Assets receive a stepped-up cost basis at death.

name: Life Insurance Company
process_summary: Call the insurance company's claims department. They will send a death claim form. Complete it and return with a certified death certificate. If the deceased was the only named beneficiary of an account, the proceeds become part of the estate. Named beneficiaries receive funds directly.
important_notes: If the policy was recently issued and the cause of death relates to information on the application, the insurer may investigate (contestability period is usually 2 years). Life insurance proceeds are generally income-tax free.

name: Mortgage Servicer
process_summary: Call the servicer's loss mitigation or estate department. Federal law (the Garn-St. Germain Act) prevents lenders from calling the loan due immediately simply because the borrower died. Heirs have time to decide whether to assume the loan, refinance, or sell. Continue making payments during this process to protect the property.
important_notes: The estate (not heirs personally) is responsible for the mortgage. If the home will be sold, a real estate attorney or agent can help manage the process.

name: Credit Card Issuer
process_summary: Call the number on the back of the card and ask for the estate or bereavement department. Notify them of the death, request a final statement, and cancel the card. The estate is responsible for the outstanding balance — do not pay from personal funds. Creditors must file claims through the probate estate.
important_notes: Authorized users on the account are not responsible for the debt. Joint cardholders are responsible. Keep records of all credit card balances — these are estate debts.
